behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module wiring (manifest parser + transitive resolver + request assembly). The failure appears at handoff boundaries, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess in this behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but behavior text says only extraction/inclusion from manifest; it does not require real API/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components together against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and transitive deps, then assert the produced vulnerability-check input includes the full expected dependency set.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real external API behavior/contract. MOCK-HIDE: a stub can trivially return correct range-matched data and miss real API semantics (range parsing, normalization, server-side matching). REAL-INFRA: this behavior is about actual query/result matching against the vulnerability database service, so real HTTP/API interaction is the minimum to expose true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner query against the real vulnerability API (or production-equivalent endpoint) with controlled dependency/version-range fixtures and assert returned CVEs match the exact range constraints, not name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: pure deterministic sorting logic. No module boundary or I/O is required to trigger the failure. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System were considered, but behavior text is only ordering rules; no real infra or cross-module interaction is necessary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorting function with an unsorted in-memory CVE list spanning severities and CVSS ties, then assert exact output order by severity rank and descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module wiring (parse -> update -> serialize). Real failures commonly appear in round-trip interactions between parser/editor/serializer, not isolated logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real network/subprocess/OS primitive beyond local module behavior; no AI dependency. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but behavior text is about content correctness of manifest mutation, which is catchable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run the manifest update pipeline on representative manifest fixtures and assert only the targeted dependency version token changes while all other dependency entries remain byte-for-byte unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real external system behavior (VCS hosting API/repository state). Behavior explicitly says 'real repository', so mocks are insufficient for minimum failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: fake PR clients can pass while real auth/branch/diff/PR API semantics fail.
behaviors.test_description: Perform an end-to-end run against a real test repository, then verify a PR is actually created and its diff contains exactly one dependency-version change and no unrelated file modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: pure semver comparison logic. No external I/O or module orchestration is required to expose misclassification. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System were considered, but behavior text is strictly semver rule evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Test the semver comparison function with in-memory version pairs (major/minor/patch/prerelease cases) and assert only major bumps are flagged as breaking per policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: module interaction/control-flow (breaking-change detector -> PR creator gate -> output reporter). Failure appears in cross-module handoff and orchestration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real AI output, no multi-agent chain, and real external PR API is not required to prove 'skip'. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but behavior text requires PR creation be skipped, which is verifiable with an in-process PR client spy plus output assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner orchestration with a breaking-change input, assert PR creation path is not invoked, and assert scanner output includes the expected review-required flag/state.
